item.title: Quebec announces July 2026 intake for family sponsorship, exempts adult dependent children from cap
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Quebec, family sponsorship, intake, applications, permanent residence

Summary: Quebec will re-open its intake for capped family sponsorship applications on July 2, 2026. Over a span of two years, the province will accept a maximum of 15,700 applications for individuals wishing to sponsor family members for permanent residence. The breakdown of these applications includes 13,300 specifically for spouses and 2,400 designated for parents or grandparents. Notably, the rules regarding dependent children have changed; dependent children aged 18 or older will no longer be subject to the same sponsorship restrictions as before. This adjustment aims to streamline the application process and address the needs of families wishing to reunite in Quebec. The provincial government is likely allowing this higher intake to accommodate the significant demand for family reunification within the province. Overall, this initiative reflects Quebec's ongoing commitment to fostering family ties and supporting its residents in bringing their loved ones to join them in Canada. Potential sponsors and applicants are encouraged to prepare for the intake period to ensure timely submission of their applications for consideration.

Quebec will re-open intake for capped family sponsorship applications on July 2, 2026. Over a two-year period, the province will accept a maximum of 15,700 applications for undertakings to sponsor family members for permanent residence: 13,300 for spouses, and 2,400 for parents or grandparents. Dependent children aged 18 or older will no longer be subject […]

The post Quebec announces July 2026 intake for family sponsorship, exempts adult dependent children from cap first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Express Entry pool gets more top heavy
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, profiles, candidates, growth rate, competitive

Summary: From May 24 to June 21, the trends in the Express Entry system revealed significant growth in top-ranking profiles. The overall pool experienced a modest increase of 0.33%, illustrating the gradual rise of candidates within the system. In sharp contrast, the number of candidates scoring 501 or higher surged impressively, escalating by 14.64%. This dramatic uptick indicates a competitive edge among individuals aiming for high-ranking profiles in the Express Entry system, showcasing a growth rate that is 4,400% higher than the overall pool. Such statistics reflect a shifting dynamic in the labor market and immigration landscape, as more candidates strive to enhance their scores and improve their chances for selection. The focus on scores within the 501–1,200 range suggests a nuanced competition, emphasizing the importance for prospective candidates to evaluate their own profiles and understand the criteria that distinguish top contenders. By assessing their competitiveness, individuals may strategize effectively to bolster their positions within the Express Entry framework, positioning themselves favorably in the selection process for permanent residency in Canada.

From May 24 to June 21, the number of top-scoring Express Entry profiles grew at a rate 4,400% faster than the overall pool. The overall Express Entry pool grew by 0.33%, while the number of candidates scoring 501 or higher increased by 14.64%. See how competitive your Express Entry profile is The 501–1,200 range accounted […]

The post Express Entry pool gets more top heavy first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Canada holds largest Canadian Experience Class draw since March
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Immigration, Express Entry, ITA, CRS score, Canadian Experience Class

Summary: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has recently conducted a draw through the Express Entry system, issuing a total of 4,000 Invitations to Apply (ITAs) specifically for candidates under the Canadian Experience Class. This draw identified candidates who possess a minimum Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score of 516. To be eligible for consideration in this round, candidates had to ensure that their Express Entry profiles were created prior to the draw. The issuance of these ITAs reflects the government's ongoing focus on attracting skilled individuals with Canadian experience, which plays a critical role in meeting labor market demands across the country. This latest round of invitations underscores the importance of the Express Entry system in Canada’s immigration strategy, enabling qualified candidates to seek permanent residency in the nation. Candidates aiming for success in future draws are encouraged to enhance their CRS scores through various means, such as improving language proficiency, gaining additional work experience, or obtaining a provincial nomination, which can significantly boost their chances of receiving an ITA. Overall, the draw is a key step for many aspiring immigrants looking to contribute to Canadian society.

Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has issued more invitations to apply (ITA) through the Express Entry system. The immigration department issued 4,000 ITAs in a draw for Canadian Experience Class candidates. To be considered in this draw, candidates needed to have: A minimum Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score of 516; and Created an Express Entry candidate […]

The post Canada holds largest Canadian Experience Class draw since March first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: 10 Proven Resume Tips for Immigrants (and CELPIP test takers) to Canada That Can Accelerate Your Job Search Success
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Canada, job search, skilled immigrants, qualifications, challenges

Summary: Moving to Canada presents an exciting opportunity for skilled immigrants, but the journey to securing a first job can be daunting. Many newcomers arrive with exceptional qualifications and extensive work experience, yet they often face significant challenges in obtaining interview calls. One key factor that contributes to this difficulty is the importance of effective communication skills, particularly conversational English. It is crucial for immigrants to practice their language proficiency, not only for the CELPIP test but also for job interviews, as clear communication can significantly impact their chances of being hired. Additionally, understanding the job market landscape, networking, and tailoring resumes to meet Canadian standards are essential steps for improving job prospects. Overcoming barriers, such as unfamiliarity with local hiring practices and cultural differences, is vital for professionals seeking employment in their respective fields. By addressing these challenges head-on, skilled immigrants can enhance their chances of success and make a smoother transition into the Canadian workforce.

Moving to Canada is an exciting opportunity, but finding your first job can be one of the biggest challenges. Many skilled immigrants arrive with impressive qualifications and years of experience, yet struggle to get interview calls (remember to practice your conversational English, for your CELPIP test as well as your job interviews!). In many cases, […]

The post 10 Proven Resume Tips for Immigrants (and CELPIP test takers) to Canada That Can Accelerate Your Job Search Success first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Federal–Provincial–Territorial Immigration Ministers meet to discuss sustainable immigration
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: FMRI, Immigration Levels Plan, Provincial Nominee Programs, labour shortages, PNP allocations

Summary: The Forum of Ministers Responsible for Immigration (FMRI) convened to discuss the 2027–2029 Immigration Levels Plan and to foster collaboration on federal–provincial–territorial (FPT) priorities. A significant focus of the meeting was on returning immigration to sustainable levels, taking into account various regional, rural, and northern contexts. The ministers acknowledged the pressing need to address labour shortages that affect key economic sectors across Canada. They emphasized the importance of supporting communities nationwide in their immigration strategies. Additionally, the role of Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) was highlighted as crucial in meeting these goals. The ministers discussed the necessity for stable and adequate allocations of PNPs to enhance their effectiveness in addressing local labour market needs. Overall, the meeting aimed to align immigration goals with the economic and demographic realities of different regions, ensuring a balanced approach that benefits all Canadians. The discussions will help shape a collaborative framework for future immigration policies that respond to both national needs and local conditions.
The Forum of Ministers Responsible for Immigration (FMRI) met today to advance collaboration on the 2027–2029 Immigration Levels Plan and discuss federal–provincial–territorial (FPT) priorities. They discussed returning immigration to sustainable levels while considering regional, rural and northern contexts, addressing labour shortages, reinforcing key economic sectors, and supporting communities across the country. Ministers also discussed the role of Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) in advancing these considerations and the need for stable and adequate PNP allocations.

item.title: Federal–Provincial–Territorial Immigration Ministers meet to discuss sustainable ... - Canada.ca
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, regional labour market, newcomer outcomes, PT ministers, improvement

Summary: The discussion focused on enhancing the Express Entry system to better align with regional labour market needs. Provincial and territorial (PT) ministers suggested several methods aimed at ensuring that the immigration process is more responsive to local demands. They highlighted the importance of tailoring immigration pathways to support economic growth and address specific workforce shortages in various regions. The ministers emphasized the necessity of improving newcomer outcomes by facilitating better integration into the community and job market. This includes providing adequate support services and resources to help newcomers settle and thrive. Recommendations were made to streamline application processes and enhance communication between federal and provincial governments to achieve these goals. The PT ministers underscored the vital role that immigration plays in rejuvenating local economies and supporting demographic stability. They collectively called for greater flexibility within the Express Entry system that would allow provinces to attract immigrants who meet their unique labour market needs. Overall, the discussions aimed at not only improving the immigration system but also ensuring that it effectively benefits both newcomers and the communities they enter.
They discussed ways to improve the Express Entry system to better address regional labour market needs and newcomer outcomes. PT ministers ...

item.title: Quebec announces July 2026 intake for family sponsorship, exempts adult dependent ...
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Canada, immigration, Express Entry, newsletter, stories

Summary: The article invites readers to subscribe to a free newsletter for updates on Canada's top immigration stories. One of the highlighted areas is the Express Entry system, which is a key pathway for skilled workers seeking to immigrate to Canada. The Express Entry pool is designed to manage applications for permanent residence from skilled immigrants. It utilizes a points-based system, assessing candidates based on factors such as age, education, work experience, and language proficiency. The newsletter aims to keep subscribers informed about changes and updates in immigration policies, trends, and success stories of applicants. By staying informed through the newsletter, potential immigrants can better navigate the complex immigration landscape of Canada. Featured articles and insights may include expert opinions, tips for improving one's chances in the Express Entry selection process, and profiles of those who have successfully settled in Canada. The newsletter serves as a valuable resource for anyone interested in immigrating to Canada, particularly those looking to enter the Express Entry pool.
Join our free newsletter. Get Canada's top immigration stories delivered to your inbox. Subscribe. More in Express Entry. Express Entry pool ...

item.title: Quebec Reopens Family Sponsorship Applications With 15700-Application Cap
item.content:encoded:
Overview, Express Entry, Skilled Immigration, CRS, Draws 2026

Summary: The Express Entry system is a pathway for skilled immigrants to become permanent residents in Canada. It operates through a points-based Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) that assesses candidates based on factors like age, education, work experience, and language proficiency. The system periodically conducts draws, inviting the highest-ranking candidates to apply for permanent residency. In 2026, additional draws may take place, providing more opportunities for skilled workers. This approach aims to meet labor market needs and support economic growth. Candidates are encouraged to improve their CRS scores by obtaining higher language test results, additional education, or obtaining work experience, thereby enhancing their chances of being selected in future draws. The Express Entry program continues to evolve, adapting to immigration goals, labor shortages, and demographic changes in Canada. Understanding the nuances of the CRS and staying updated on draw results and criteria are essential for prospective applicants to navigate the immigration process successfully. As the program develops, it remains a vital tool for both Canada and skilled workers seeking to contribute to the country’s economy and society.
... Overview · Express Entry Overview · Express Entry for Skilled Immigration · Express Entry Draws in 2026 · Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) · Express ...

item.title: Express Entry pool gets more top heavy | CIC News
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, top-scoring profiles, growth rate, overall pool, immigration

Summary: The number of top-scoring Express Entry profiles experienced an unprecedented growth rate of 4,400% from May 24 to June 21, significantly outpacing the overall pool of profiles. This surge indicates a substantial increase in high-ranking candidates for immigration under Canada's Express Entry system. While the overall pool also saw growth, it occurred at a much slower pace, highlighting the competitive nature of the immigration landscape. The rise in top-scoring profiles may point to increased interest in Canadian immigration programs or possibly a response to changes in immigration policies during that period. Additionally, this trend could affect application processing times and selection criteria, potentially benefiting skilled workers and experienced professionals looking to settle in Canada. The dramatic shift in profile numbers suggests that both candidates and immigration authorities should prepare for further changes and trends in the coming months. Overall, the rapid growth of top-scoring profiles confirms the dynamic nature of the Express Entry system and remains an important area for future monitoring by prospective immigrants and stakeholders in the immigration sector.
From May 24 to June 21, the number of top-scoring Express Entry profiles grew at a rate 4,400% faster than the overall pool.

item.title: Express Entry CEC Draw of 4000- CRS Drops to 516 - YouTube
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: CEC draw, Invitations to Apply, IRCC, CRS score, 4000

Summary: The Canadian Experience Class (CEC) draw is proceeding as planned, with the latest round resulting in the issuance of 4,000 Invitations to Apply (ITAs) for permanent residency. This draw reflects the ongoing commitment of the Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) to facilitate immigration and address labor shortages in the country. The Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score in the latest draw decreased to 516, down from 518 in the previous round. This minor drop in score indicates a slight easing of competition among candidates, potentially allowing more individuals to qualify for immigration. Such draws are crucial for maintaining a steady influx of skilled workers, which is vital for Canada's economic growth and recovery, especially post-pandemic. The IRCC's approach underscores the importance of attracting talent with Canadian work experience, thereby benefiting both the immigrants and the Canadian economy. As immigration policies continue to evolve, prospective applicants are encouraged to stay informed about upcoming draws and changes in the CRS scoring system, maximizing their chances to secure an ITA in future rounds.
CEC draw is back on track. IRCC issued 4000 Invitations to Apply today, with the CRS score dropping to 516 from 518 in the previous draw.

item.title: IRCC updates police certificate requirements for International Experience Canada work permits
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Canada, police certificate, International Experience Canada, work permits, immigration department

Summary: Canada has updated its police certificate requirements for foreign nationals applying for work permits through the International Experience Canada (IEC) program. The changes reflect new instructions issued to immigration officers, which can be found on the immigration department's official website. One key aspect of these updated guidelines is the need for a police certificate from the applicant's current country of residence. This clarification aims to streamline the application process and ensure that applicants present the necessary documentation. The updated requirements are intended to enhance security measures and maintain the integrity of the work permit application process. As applicants prepare for their IEC applications, they are advised to be aware of these changes and ensure they have the proper police verification documentation in place. Additionally, to help navigate the updated application process, potential applicants are encouraged to schedule a free work permit consultation to get expert guidance tailored to their specific situation. This move is part of Canada's effort to facilitate international mobility while ensuring safety and compliance with immigration regulations.

Canada has clarified police certificate requirements for foreign nationals applying for work permits through the International Experience Canada (IEC) program. The clarifications come in the form of updated instructions to officers published on the immigration department’s website. Schedule a Free Work Permit Consultation The updated instructions specify that the applicant’s police certificate for their current […]

The post IRCC updates police certificate requirements for International Experience Canada work permits first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: How a Canadian great-grandparent can make you a citizen
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Canadian, citizen, great-grandparent, apply, passport

Summary: If one of your great-grandparents was a Canadian citizen, you may qualify for Canadian citizenship regardless of where you, your parents, or your grandparents were born. This allows individuals who have no recent ties to Canada, such as holding a Canadian passport in generations, the opportunity to connect with their heritage. To gain citizenship, you need to establish your relationship with your Canadian great-grandparent through appropriate documentation. This process can provide you with the chance to become a Canadian citizen and access the rights and privileges associated with it. Once you gather the necessary proof of lineage, you can proceed to apply for citizenship. Being aware of this possibility can open doors for individuals seeking to explore their family history and gain Canadian nationality, thus potentially benefiting from Canada's resources, services, and community as citizens. This citizenship pathway emphasizes the significance of familial connections and may be an avenue for many who wish to embrace their Canadian roots.

If one of your great-grandparents was Canadian, you may now be a Canadian citizen—even if you, your parents, and your grandparents were all born abroad, and no one in the family has held a Canadian passport in generations. If you can prove your relation to your Canadian great-grandparent, you can apply for the document that […]

The post How a Canadian great-grandparent can make you a citizen first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Provincial nominees receive invitations in first Express Entry draw since May
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Immigration, ITA, Express Entry, CRS, PNP

Summary: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has recently issued 955 invitations to apply (ITA) through the Express Entry system, specifically targeting candidates in the Provincial Nominee Program (PNP). This draw was significant as it opened opportunities for individuals looking to immigrate to Canada under the PNP pathway. To be eligible for this draw, candidates were required to have a minimum Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score of 730, which ensures that only those with higher qualifications and potential for success in Canada were considered. The Express Entry system is a vital component of Canada’s immigration strategy, aiming to attract skilled workers to meet labor market demands. It not only facilitates application processing but also allows for a more streamlined immigration experience. This latest draw highlights the ongoing commitment of the IRCC to invite skilled individuals and support provincial economies by filling labor shortages. Candidates who meet the criteria and receive an ITA will have the opportunity to apply for permanent residency in Canada, marking a significant step toward building their future in the country.

Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) has issued more invitations to apply (ITA) through the Express Entry system. The immigration department issued 955 ITAs in a draw for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) candidates. To be considered in this draw, candidates needed to have: A minimum Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score of 730; and Created an […]

The post Provincial nominees receive invitations in first Express Entry draw since May first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Canada gives second chance to asylum seekers from Uganda
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Uganda, Canada, protected person status, negative decision, measures

Summary: On June 19, new measures were announced that will benefit Ugandan nationals and former residents of Uganda facing imminent removal from Canada. The initiative aims to provide these individuals with a second chance to obtain protected person status. Specifically, it applies to those who received a final negative decision regarding their application between June 20, 2025, and June 19, 2026. This move is part of Canada's efforts to address the unique challenges and risks faced by Ugandans, particularly given the political and social climate in Uganda. Eligible individuals will have the opportunity to reapply and present their cases for consideration under these new regulations. This initiative reflects Canada's commitment to providing protection and support to individuals in need, ensuring that those who may have legitimate claims to refugee status are not unfairly removed from the country. The measures demonstrate the government’s responsive approach to changing circumstances and underlying human rights issues in Uganda, reaffirming Canada’s role in global humanitarian efforts.

Many individuals from Uganda who had faced imminent removal from Canada will be given a second chance at obtaining protected person status. New measures announced on June 19 apply to Ugandan nationals and former residents of Uganda who received a final negative decision between June 20, 2025, and June 19, 2026, on one of the […]

The post Canada gives second chance to asylum seekers from Uganda first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Prince Edward Island holds largest provincial immigration draw this year
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Prince Edward Island, PNP, candidates, nomination, high-demand

Summary: On June 18, Prince Edward Island conducted its largest provincial nominee program (PNP) draw of 2026, inviting a total of 182 candidates to apply for provincial nomination. This draw specifically targeted candidates who are already residing in the province and are currently engaged in high-demand occupations and sectors. The focus on in-province candidates highlights the province's strategy to address labor shortages by selecting individuals who are already integrated into the local community and workforce. The Prince Edward Island Provincial Nominee Program offers various pathways for individuals wishing to settle in the province, and the recent draw illustrates a commitment to attracting skilled workers who can contribute to the local economy. The decision to invite candidates from high-demand occupations reflects the province's ongoing effort to align its immigration strategies with labor market needs. By issuing Invitations to Apply (ITA) to these candidates, the PEI PNP aims to streamline the nomination process and help in resolving workforce challenges within the province. This initiative marks a significant step in enhancing the provincial immigration framework and facilitates the growth of key sectors in Prince Edward Island.

On June 18, Prince Edward Island held its largest draw of 2026, inviting 182 candidates to apply for provincial nomination. Invitations were extended to in-province candidates working in high-demand occupations and sectors. Assess your eligibility for enhanced PNP streams Draw details The Prince Edward Island Provincial Nominee Program (PEI PNP) issued invitations to apply (ITA) […]

The post Prince Edward Island holds largest provincial immigration draw this year first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: What I Say, or How I Say It? Ethnic Accents and Hiring Evaluations in the Greater Toronto Area
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, Year-end Report, 2020, White Listeners, Sounding Black

Summary: This report focuses on the Express Entry system's performance in 2020, analyzing data and trends related to immigration. It highlights the challenges faced during the year, particularly due to the COVID-19 pandemic, which led to changes in processing times and applicant numbers. Despite these obstacles, the report notes a commitment to maintaining high standards for the selected immigrants, emphasizing the importance of a diverse and skilled workforce in Canada. Furthermore, it conveys statistics indicating the demographics of applicants, reflecting the country's intention to attract individuals who can fill labor shortages and contribute to the economy. The report also recognizes the importance of feedback from both applicants and stakeholders to improve the Express Entry system. Lastly, it discusses prospective adjustments for future years to enhance efficiency and responsiveness, aiming to adapt to evolving socio-economic conditions and immigration goals. The analysis serves as a reflection on past achievements and a roadmap for future enhancements in the immigration process.
... express-entry-year-end-report-2020.html.Google Scholar. Johnson, Fern L., and Buttny, Richard. 1982. White listeners' responses to 'sounding black ...

item.title: Canada gives second chance to asylum seekers from Uganda - CIC News
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, asylum seekers, Canada, immigration, Helmcken Falls

Summary: The Canadian Express Entry program has recently faced delays, with the last draw occurring in May, affecting the immigration process for many hopeful candidates. In an effort to support those seeking refuge, Canada has announced a new initiative offering a second chance for asylum seekers who have been denied refugee status. This move is part of Canada’s commitment to human rights and providing sanctuary to individuals facing persecution in their home countries. The picturesque Helmcken Falls in British Columbia represents the natural beauty that many immigrants aspire to experience in their new lives. The Canadian government continues to evolve its immigration policies, reflecting the growing need for a balanced approach that addresses humanitarian concerns while also managing the country's immigration levels. The changes in the Express Entry system and the support for asylum seekers demonstrate Canada's dedication to creating a diverse and inclusive society. As the landscape of immigration continues to shift, potential immigrants are encouraged to stay informed about new developments and opportunities available within the Canadian immigration framework.
... Express Entry draw since May · Express Entry · A picture of Helmcken Falls in British Columbia · Canada gives second chance to asylum seekers from ...

item.title: First Express Entry Draw Of June 2026 Sent 955 PR Invitations
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, Immigration, Canada, candidates, draw

Summary: Today, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) held the first Express Entry draw of June 2026. The draw specifically targeted candidates who possess certain qualifications or skills deemed essential for Canada's workforce. The selection criteria focused on various factors such as job experience, language proficiency, and education levels to ensure that the candidates can contribute effectively to the Canadian economy. This draw aligns with Canada's ongoing commitment to attracting skilled immigrants to fill labor shortages in various sectors. Participants who meet the eligibility requirements submitted their profiles to the Express Entry pool, where they are ranked against one another based on the Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS). Successful candidates receive Invitations to Apply (ITAs) for permanent residency, which enables them to settle in Canada permanently. The draw serves as an opportunity for skilled workers from around the globe to become part of Canada's diverse and growing community. The IRCC is expected to continue these regular draws to facilitate immigration and support the nation's economic growth through skilled immigration.
Today, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada conducted the first Express Entry draw of June 2026, targeting candidates who hold a ...

item.title: Prince Edward Island holds largest provincial immigration draw this year - CIC News
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, international students, Labour Impact, PEI, immigration

Summary: The article discusses the Express Entry streams available for immigrants in Canada, focusing particularly on international student graduates. It highlights the pathways provided by the Labour Impact stream, designed to facilitate the transition of skilled workers into the Canadian workforce. Additionally, the article mentions the PEI Express Entry program which targets candidates who have the skills and experience needed in the province of Prince Edward Island. Important dates relevant to these programs include February 19, 2026, and March 20, 2026, indicating periods for potential applications or changes in regulations. Overall, the piece emphasizes the opportunities for international students and skilled workers looking to settle in Canada through various immigration streams, including those that are aligned with provincial needs and labor market demands. The article serves as a guide for applicants to understand their options and the timelines involved in the immigration process.
... Express Entry streams. International student graduates from the ... *Labour Impact and PEI Express Entry. February 19, 2026, 109. March 20, 2026 ...

item.title: Latest PEI PNP Draw of 2026 Invites 182 Foreign Workers - cic times
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: PEI PNP, draw, invitations, Labour stream, Express Entry

Summary: The latest Prince Edward Island (PEI) Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) draw took place on June 18, 2026. During this draw, a total of 182 invitations were issued to applicants under two main categories: the Labour stream and the Express Entry stream. This draw reflects the province's ongoing efforts to attract skilled workers and support its labor market needs. The Labour stream primarily targets candidates with work experience in PEI or who possess specific skills required by local employers. Meanwhile, the Express Entry stream is connected to Canada’s federal immigration system, allowing the province to nominate qualified candidates from the federal pool. This number of invitations represents a strategic move by the PEI government to enhance community growth and ensure a steady influx of skilled professionals. Interested candidates are encouraged to keep an eye on future draws, as these invitations are a vital component of the immigration process in PEI, fostering economic development and addressing labor shortages in various sectors. The inclusion of diverse communities further enriches the province's cultural tapestry while promoting sustained economic progress.
The latest PEI PNP draw held on June 18, 2026, issued 182 invitations under the Labour and Express Entry stream. Check the latest PEI PNP draw ...

item.title: How to request urgent processing of a Canadian citizenship certificate
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: proof of citizenship, IRCC, processing time, Bill C-3, Canadian

Summary: A proof of citizenship certificate is an important document for confirming Canadian citizenship. The processing time for this certificate through Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) currently stands at approximately 15 months. This lengthy wait time has increased recently due to a surge in applications. A significant number of applicants are American citizens who have become newly eligible for Canadian citizenship under Bill C-3, which took effect in December 2025. As a result, the demand for proof of citizenship has escalated, leading to a growing queue of applicants. The influx of applications reflects the changing landscape of citizenship eligibility in Canada, particularly driven by recent legislative changes. Individuals seeking to verify their Canadian citizenship are now facing a much longer waiting period, which highlights the pressing need for IRCC to address processing delays and improve service efficiency. Overall, the situation emphasizes the importance of being informed about the citizenship application process and the potential timelines involved.

A proof of citizenship certificate is the document that confirms you’re already Canadian. IRCC currently takes about 15 months to process one, and that wait has climbed as hundreds of thousands of people, many of them Americans newly eligible under Bill C-3, have joined the queue since the law took effect in December 2025. For […]

The post How to request urgent processing of a Canadian citizenship certificate first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Manitoba issues invitations for provincial immigration under strategic recruitment initiative
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Manitoba, Provincial Nominee Program, skilled workers, invitations, strategic recruitment

Summary: On June 18, 2026, the Manitoba Provincial Nominee Program (MPNP) issued invitations to apply for provincial nomination to a total of 124 candidates. These invitations were specifically extended under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba and Skilled Worker Overseas pathways. The candidates invited in this round indicated that they were either directly recruited by the MPNP as part of a strategic recruitment initiative or that they met specific eligibility criteria outlined by the program. The MPNP aims to address labor market shortages in Manitoba by attracting skilled workers to the province. This initiative underscores the importance of targeted recruitment efforts that align with the province's labor market needs. The program provides a pathway for skilled workers to apply for permanent residency, contributing to the growth and sustainability of Manitoba's economy. The systematic approach taken by the MPNP facilitates a streamlined process for potential immigrants to integrate and thrive within the Manitoba community. This round of invitations reflects ongoing efforts to enhance the skilled labor pool and promote economic development in the region.

On June 18, 2026, the Manitoba Provincial Nominee Program (MPNP) issued invitations to apply for provincial nomination to 124 candidates under the Skilled Worker in Manitoba and Skilled Worker Overseas pathways. Candidates invited in this round had declared that they were directly invited by the MPNP as part of a strategic recruitment initiative, or that […]

The post Manitoba issues invitations for provincial immigration under strategic recruitment initiative first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: New Student and Worker Arrivals to Canada Drop 73 Percent - ImmigCanada
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: temporary residents, PNP, Express Entry, TR-to-PR, transitions

Summary: Temporary residents in Canada are currently evaluating their pathways to permanent residency and may find that provincial nomination programs (PNPs) are more advantageous than the federal Express Entry system at this time. Recent trends indicate that the transition from temporary resident (TR) to permanent resident (PR) has shown a significant flow through PNPs, especially as provinces are actively seeking to attract and retain skilled workers. Many provinces have launched specific streams targeting temporary residents, offering them tailored opportunities that align with local labor market needs. The processing times for PNP applications can sometimes be quicker than Express Entry, making PNPs an attractive option for those looking to secure permanent residency efficiently. Additionally, candidates with provincial nomination can receive enhanced points in the Express Entry system, benefiting their overall applications. Therefore, those on temporary permits, such as work or study permits, should closely examine the PNP options available in their province, as these may provide a more streamlined and effective routing to permanent residency compared to the federated system's requirements. Engaging with local immigration resources can also help in making informed decisions.
Should temporary residents in Canada prioritize PNP over federal Express Entry right now? Many of the recent TR-to-PR transitions are flowing through ...

item.title: Canada's Express Entry 2026: Full List of Jobs and Categories Prioritised for Permanent Residence
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Canada, Express Entry, work requirements, priority jobs, skilled workers

Summary: Canada's 2026 Express Entry system is set to implement stricter work requirements for skilled workers aiming to immigrate. This initiative is part of a broader strategy to align immigration with labor market needs and ensure that new immigrants possess the necessary skills to contribute effectively to the Canadian economy. In addition to tightening these work requirements, the Express Entry system will introduce new priority job categories, particularly focusing on sectors such as healthcare and research. This effort seeks to attract talent in fields where there is a current demand for skilled labor. By creating these priority job categories, the Canadian government aims to streamline the immigration process for professionals in critical areas, thereby addressing workforce shortages and enhancing the country's overall productivity. Furthermore, the changes highlight Canada's commitment to supporting sectors that are fundamental for national growth and public well-being. Overall, the 2026 enhancements to the Express Entry framework reflect the challenges and opportunities within the Canadian workforce, making it more responsive to emerging needs in the labor market.
Canada's 2026 Express Entry tightens work requirements while introducing new priority job categories for skilled workers in healthcare, research, ...

item.title: PGWP Approved With Positive Client Feedback | Canada Immigration Success Story
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: IRCC, Express Entry, Canada, immigration, updates

Summary: The IRCC has announced significant changes to the Express Entry system for the years 2026 and 2027, aiming to streamline the immigration process to Canada. These updates will likely include new criteria and point allocation methods that prioritize candidates based on the labor market needs of various provinces. The government is focusing on attracting skilled workers to fill specific job shortages in industries critical to the Canadian economy. Moreover, there may be enhancements in the processing times for applications, benefiting both applicants and the immigration system overall. The IRCC is also expected to implement additional support mechanisms for newcomers, ensuring they integrate smoothly into Canadian society. Transparency in the selection process is anticipated with more frequent updates and insights on changes, allowing applicants to better prepare their submissions. These amendments reflect Canada’s ongoing commitment to welcoming diverse talent and maintaining an efficient immigration system that supports economic growth and social cohesion. Stakeholders and potential immigrants are encouraged to stay informed and engaged with these changes as they unfold, understanding that the landscape of Canadian immigration is evolving to meet modern needs.
IRCC Explains CHANGES in 2026-2027 EXPRESS ENTRY - Canada Immigration News Latest IRCC Updates. Sahil Sayal•24K views · 15:53 · Go to channel The ...

item.title: After cabinet entry, IUML looks to spread roots | The New Indian Express Chennai - Magzter
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: TVK-led government, excitement, entry, political strategy, developments

Summary: The story discusses the excitement surrounding a political party's recent entry into the TVK-led government. This development has energized the party's members and supporters, who view it as a significant opportunity to influence governance. The party's leadership believes that their participation in the government will allow them to implement key policies and initiatives. Additionally, the narrative explores the strategic moves made by both the party and the TVK leadership to ensure a successful collaboration. The political landscape is shifting, and with it, new alliances are forming, leading to changes in public opinion and voter dynamics. The article highlights the challenges and opportunities that come with being part of a ruling coalition while also addressing the expectations that citizens have for effective governance. Overall, this story encapsulates the optimism and strategic planning that surrounds the party's new role in the government, underscoring the implications for future political developments in the region. The excitement is palpable as members look forward to making a tangible impact in governance and fulfilling their promises to the electorate.
Read this exciting story from The New Indian Express Chennai June 21, 2026 issue. BUOYED by its entry into the TVK-led government, and officially ...

item.title: Canada is already returning citizenship certificates it demanded days ago from lost Canadians
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Canadians, citizenship, certificates, review, reversal

Summary: Recently, Canada's citizenship department requested some newly naturalized Canadians to surrender their citizenship certificates for review due to flagged applications. This decision caused uncertainty among the affected individuals, who were concerned about their status as citizens. However, less than a week later, in a surprising turnaround, the government began notifying many of those individuals that they could retain their citizenship certificates. This quick reversal has led to some confusion among applicants, emphasizing the government's reassessment of the situation. The citizenship department's initial request was likely driven by concerns over the integrity of some applications, but the subsequent decision to issue "revalidation" for certain individuals suggests that many may not have been affected as initially thought. This incident highlights the complexities and challenges that can arise in the citizenship application process, especially when it comes to ensuring eligibility and integrity among applicants. Ultimately, the swift change in direction aims to restore confidence in the citizenship process while addressing the concerns of those newly naturalized Canadians who were impacted by the initial request.

Some of the new Canadians asked to surrender their citizenship certificates last week have already been told they can keep them, in a quick reversal by Canada’s citizenship department. Less than a week after the federal government asked flagged applicants to hand back their certificates for review, it has begun reversing those requests, issuing “revalidation […]

The post Canada is already returning citizenship certificates it demanded days ago from lost Canadians first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: British Columbia extends over 270 invitations to high economic impact candidates in latest draw
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: British Columbia, Provincial Nominee Program, invitations, candidates, job offer

Summary: On June 18, the British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program (BCPNP) issued a total of 279 invitations to high economic impact candidates for provincial nomination. The candidates who were invited qualified under one of two selection factors: either their wage or salary associated with a job offer classified under TEER (Training, Education, Experience and Responsibilities) 0–3, or their profile registration score. This reflects the program's focus on attracting individuals who can significantly contribute to the province’s economy through skilled employment. The BCPNP allows the provincial government to address specific labor market needs by selecting candidates who are most likely to succeed and integrate into the workforce. The invitations are part of ongoing efforts by the BCPNP to manage the immigration process effectively, targeting high-skilled workers in key sectors. Candidates who received invitations can now apply for provincial nomination, which can enhance their prospects for permanent residency in Canada. This round of invitations marks another step forward in the province’s strategy to attract qualified individuals who meet economic demands.

On June 18, the British Columbia Provincial Nominee Program (BCPNP) invited a total of 279 high economic impact candidates to apply for provincial nomination. Invitations were issued to candidates who qualified under one of two selection factors: wage or salary tied to a TEER 0–3 job offer, or profile registration score. Assess your eligibility for […]

The post British Columbia extends over 270 invitations to high economic impact candidates in latest draw first appeared on CIC News.

item.title: Canada's Express Entry 2026: Full List Of Jobs And Categories Prioritised For Permanent Residence
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Canada, Express Entry, skilled workers, key sectors, pathways

Summary: Canada's 2026 Express Entry program is set to prioritize skilled workers from crucial sectors such as healthcare, technology, and trades. The government aims to address labor shortages and skill gaps by streamlining immigration pathways for individuals with expertise in these areas. This new approach is designed to enhance the efficiency of the immigration system, making it easier for qualified candidates to settle in Canada. The updated Express Entry will likely include a points-based system that evaluates applicants based on their qualifications, work experience, and potential contributions to the Canadian economy. In addition, the program is expected to clarify the selection criteria, ensuring that candidates have a clearer understanding of their eligibility and how to improve their chances of acceptance. By focusing on these key sectors, Canada hopes to create a more robust workforce that can meet the growing demands of its economy. Ultimately, the 2026 Express Entry initiative reflects Canada's commitment to attracting top talent from around the world while fulfilling its labor market needs.
Canada's 2026 Express Entry prioritises skilled workers in key sectors, including healthcare, technology, and trades, clarifying pathways for ...

item.title: 6 New Canada Asylum Rules And Changes Coming Soon
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Express Entry, CRS score, occupations, Canada, immigration

Summary: The article by Kamal Deep Singh discusses 37 specific occupations that are likely to receive additional Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) points under Canada’s Express Entry immigration system. These occupations are in high demand and relate to various skilled trades and professions, including healthcare, technology, engineering, and other sectors. Immigrants aiming for permanent residency through Express Entry can enhance their CRS scores, which increases their chances of receiving an Invitation to Apply (ITA). The listed occupations may include roles such as nurses, software engineers, electricians, and various skilled trades, reflecting labor shortages in Canada. Proficiency in one of these occupations not only aids in securing a job offer but also significantly boosts the applicant's overall CRS score, giving them a competitive edge in the immigration pool. The article emphasizes the importance of potential applicants keeping abreast of labor market trends and understanding how their skills align with the needs of the Canadian economy. By doing so, they can optimize their immigration strategy and increase their likelihood of success in the Express Entry system.
37 Express Entry Occupations That Could Get Extra CRS Score. by Kamal Deep Singh, RCIC ...

item.title: Most People Get This Express Entry Decision Wrong - YouTube
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: express entry, healthcare, trades, education, immigration

Summary: The Express Entry program is a key immigration pathway for skilled workers wanting to settle in Canada. It comprises several categories, including healthcare, trades, and education. The healthcare category targets professionals such as doctors, nurses, and allied health workers, streamlining their application process to address the growing demand in Canada's healthcare system. The trades category focuses on skilled tradespeople, such as electricians, plumbers, and welders, who are essential to supporting Canada's infrastructure and economic growth. The education category is geared towards international graduates and professionals with recognized qualifications, enabling them to contribute to Canada's diverse workforce. Each category has specific criteria and pathways designed to facilitate the immigration of qualified individuals, promoting a balanced and skilled labor market. Successful candidates receive a Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score, which determines their likelihood of being invited to apply for permanent residency. The program is aimed at enhancing Canada's economy while also addressing labor shortages in various sectors, ensuring a vibrant and skilled population. Overall, Express Entry serves as a comprehensive framework for attracting skilled immigrants to Canada, thereby benefiting both the economy and the community at large.
... express entry, healthcare express entry category, trades category express entry, education category express entry] Declaration: We want to make it ...

item.title: Manitoba Issues 124 Invitations to Apply Under the Skilled Worker Stream - cic times
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: Letters of Advice, Express Entry, candidates, job, draw

Summary: In a recent draw, a total of 124 Letters of Advice to Apply were issued to potential candidates. Among these, 22 letters were specifically awarded to candidates who provided a valid Express Entry profile number along with their job details. This indicates a focus on applicants who have already navigated through the Express Entry system, showcasing their qualification and alignment with the job market demands. The process of issuing these letters is part of the Canadian immigration strategy aimed at attracting skilled workers to meet labor shortages in specific sectors. Candidates who receive these letters are given the opportunity to proceed with their applications for permanent residency, emphasizing the importance of having a valid Express Entry profile. This draw demonstrates the Canadian government's ongoing commitment to facilitating immigration pathways for skilled professionals, ultimately contributing to the country's economic growth and diversity. The recent issuance of Letters of Advice aligns with a broader trend of targeted immigration policies that seek to balance labor market needs with demographic growth, thereby enhancing Canada’s workforce and global competitiveness.
Of the 124 Letters of Advice to Apply issued in this draw, 22 were issued to candidates who declared a valid Express Entry profile number and job ...

item.title: IRCC Issues More CEC Invitations In Second Express Entry Draw Of The Week Jermain ...
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: ITA, CEC, CRS score, Express Entry, November

Summary: The department issued 4,000 Invitations to Apply (ITAs) in a recent Canadian Experience Class (CEC) draw. Candidates were required to have a Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score of 521 to qualify for the invitation. This draw highlights the continuous efforts of the Canadian immigration system to invite skilled workers to contribute to the country’s economy. To be eligible for this draw, candidates needed to have created their Express Entry profiles by 2:52:14 AM UTC on the specified date in November. The timing of profile creation is critical, as only those profiles submitted before this cutoff were considered for the draw. The CRS score of 521 indicates a competitive selection process, typically reflecting the qualifications, work experience, and skills of the candidates. This draw underscores the importance of staying updated and prepared for potential applicants looking to immigrate to Canada through the Express Entry system. Overall, the CEC draw represents a significant opportunity for skilled workers to transition into permanent residency in Canada, contributing to the nation’s vibrant workforce.
The department issued 4000 ITAs in a CEC draw. Candidates needed a CRS score of 521 and an Express Entry profile created by 2:52:14 AM UTC on Nove ...

item.title: Watch Live TV On Your Desktop For Free - Zattoo Player Pedro Porro (wgQfMKNzQA)
item.content:encoded:
Keywords: express entry, Paris-SG, Cathie Wood, AI stocks, tutorials

Summary: The article discusses the recent Express Entry draw, likely related to Canadian immigration processes. It highlights insights gathered from the Paris-SG archive, suggesting a deeper analysis of trends related to the topic. Additionally, the article touches on Cathie Wood's investment strategies, focusing on AI stocks. Wood has been known for her forward-thinking investment approach, particularly in technology and innovation sectors. The post also features tutorials and tech notes, indicating a segment aimed at educating readers on various subjects, including stock trading and market analysis. The combination of these elements—immigration insights, investment strategies, and educational content—provides a comprehensive overview of the current landscape in both personal finance and immigration matters. Ultimately, it emphasizes the interconnectedness of technology, finance, and global mobility, aimed at informing individuals about opportunities and strategies they might consider in these arenas.
